lede: A look at October 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ben Shelton and David Phelps (baseball), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hurricane Milton
events.desc: Hurricane Milton makes landfall in Siesta Key, Florida, as a Category 3 hurricane, causing US$34.3 billion in damage only two weeks after Hurricane Helene impacted the state.

events.title: Malala Yousafzai
events.desc: Pakistani Taliban attempt to assassinate outspoken schoolgirl Malala Yousafzai.

events.title: The Phantom of the Opera (1986 musical)
events.desc: The Phantom of the Opera, eventually the second longest running musical in London, opens at Her Majesty's Theatre.

events.title: Khmer Republic
events.desc: The Khmer Republic is proclaimed in Cambodia.

events.title: Parliament of Finland
events.desc: The Finnish Parliament elects Prince Frederick Charles of Hesse as King of Finland, but he never accedes to the throne due to Germany's defeat in World War I.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Siege of Antwerp comes to an end.

events.title: SS Volturno (1906)
events.desc: The steamship SS Volturno catches fire in the mid-Atlantic.

events.title: Universal Postal Union
events.desc: The Universal Postal Union is created by the Treaty of Bern.

events.title: Hobart
events.desc: Hobart, capital of Tasmania, is founded.

events.title: 1740 Batavia massacre
events.desc: Dutch colonists and Javanese natives begin a massacre of the ethnic Chinese population in Batavia, eventually killing at least 10,000.

births.name: Peter Mansfield
births.line: English physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate (died 2017)

deaths.text: Solomon Lar, Nigerian educator and politician, 4th Governor of Plateau State
Carolyn Gold Heilbrun, American author and academic
Frank Castleman, American football player, baseball player, and coach

observances.text: Christian feast day: Innocencio of Mary Immaculate and Martyrs of Asturias
